hero.eyebrow: When more effort isn't the answer
hero.headline: Turn product into leverage.

offerings.label: Product leadership, at every depth
offerings.heading: The right level of product leadership — for the leap you're making.

items.title: Fractional CPO
items.subtitle: Full ownership of the product function — strategy through execution.
items.body: Own strategy, align go-to-market, and drive execution — embedded in your team without the cost or ramp of a full-time hire.
triggers: AI-native product or capabilities on the table — and no senior owner to drive it
Founder-led product — ready to hand it to a seasoned operator
Scale and complexity have outgrown current product leadership

items.title: Strategic Advisor
items.subtitle: Your team owns the build — I pressure-test direction and sharpen decisions.
items.body: Some bets are too expensive to get wrong. I sharpen direction, pressure-test key decisions, and reorient around outcomes over output — high-leverage counsel, when the stakes are highest.
triggers: You're shipping — but it's not showing up in retention or revenue
A high-stakes bet on the table: raise, new market, or strategic pivot
Execution is stalling — and the team needs a senior hand to accelerate

items.title: Executive Operator
items.subtitle: One strategic initiative — I own it from concept to launch.
items.body: What slows launches is rarely the building — it's the ability to rapidly synthesize direction and make good decisions, quickly. I own a single strategic bet end-to-end — working with your team in real time and driving design, build, and iteration to launch.
triggers: A strategic initiative on the table — and no senior owner to drive it
Delivery keeps stalling on decisions only a product executive can make
Deep technical capability that needs translating into a product people actually use

items.title: Craft Coach
items.subtitle: Raise the craft and skills of the leaders and teams already doing the work.
items.body: Working with someone who's done it compresses the curve. 1:1 coaching, hands-on workshops, and AI-native ways of working — leveling up leadership and craft for what's coming next.
triggers: Your product leader is two steps from ready — and you need them ready now
You're betting on AI-native workflows — and no one owns the transition
AI raised what's possible on execution — discovery, definition, and prototyping workflows need to match pace without losing rigor

items.label: ARR growth at Monday.com, scaled past $1B ARR
Churn reduction at BQE Software in under 10 months

about.label: The operator behind the practice
about.heading: Meet Guy Assedou.
about.quote: What drives me is helping leaders <grad>realize their vision</grad> and create real impact

points.title: The inflection point is the job
points.body: I've spent my career at transition moments — when the foundation exists but the path forward isn't clear. I step in to bring focus and make every bet move the metrics that matter.

points.title: Pattern recognition, earned
points.body: Built across B2B/B2B2C/B2C at global enterprises and early-stage startups, horizontal and vertical markets. Breadth across distinct contexts with enough reps to master each — is what turns experience into instinct.

points.title: Expertise in the US & Israeli markets
points.body: Spent a decade embedded in North American corporates and startups, with deep roots in Israel's tech ecosystem. I bring a first-hand view into user mindset, cultural context, and market dynamics.

points.title: Close to the craft
points.body: Continuously develop my craft at the forefront — I've built industry-first AI-native products at Monday.com ($1B ARR) and Cognota. I leverage AI workflows and develop dedicated frameworks. And share those — as a mentor and speaker.

about.credentials: Kellogg MBA (strategy) · Technion BS EE (engineering) · IDF Major (leadership)

items.q: How is this different from hiring a full-time CPO?
items.a: You get senior product leadership — strategy, execution, and team development — without the six-month search, 12-month ramp, equity dilution, or full-time cost. Engagements are scoped to what you actually need, and can scale up or down as the business evolves. Not sure which model fits? The 30-minute call usually makes it clear.

items.q: What does a typical engagement look like?
items.a: It depends on the engagement — and that range is intentional. A focused workshop can be a single day. Advisory retainers run month-to-month with regular weekly touchpoints. Owning a single initiative runs two to four months. Embedded fractional CPO work runs three to six months with a weekly operating cadence. If you're unsure which model fits, it usually becomes very clear during the first call.

items.q: How do you manage working with multiple clients at once?
items.a: Each engagement runs on a defined operating cadence — dedicated weekly time, not reactive availability. The structure is intentional: it forces prioritization and keeps the work high-leverage. I carry a small number of active engagements at any one time, so you get consistent, focused attention — not a fractional slice of someone stretched thin.

items.q: How do you approach AI in product strategy?
items.a: AI isn't a feature layer — it's a rethinking of what the product does and how the team operates. I help founders separate real leverage from noise: which bets to make, how to sequence them, and how to build the AI-native workflows that actually compound. I've led this work across B2B SaaS at multiple stages.

items.q: How is this different from a fractional CPO marketplace or advisory network?
items.a: Most fractional models give you access to a pool — you get matched, you get hours, you get advice. I work differently. I carry a small number of engagements at any one time, embedded in your team, and invested in your success. The difference shows up in execution: I'm not an outsider, I'm in the room where the decisions get made. I operate the way I build products — focused on outcomes.

cta.heading: Let's turn your product into leverage.
cta.body: A 30-minute call to map where product leadership creates the most impact. No pitch, no commitment.
